authors_text: Tianyu Wang,Baxi Chong,Yuelin Deng,Ruijie Fu,Howie Choset,Daniel I. Goldman
title: Generalized Omega Turn Gait Enables Agile Limbless Robot Turning in Complex Environments
title_zh: 通用 Omega 转弯步态使无肢机器人能够在复杂环境中灵活转弯
abstract: Reorientation (turning in plane) plays a critical role for all robots in any field application, especially those that in confined spaces. While important, reorientation remains a relatively unstudied problem for robots, including limbless mechanisms, often called snake robots. Instead of looking at snakes, we take inspiration from observations of the turning behavior of tiny nematode worms C. elegans. Our previous work presented an in-place and in-plane turning gait for limbless robots, called an omega turn, and prescribed it using a novel two-wave template. In this work, we advance omega turn-inspired controllers in three aspects: 1) we use geometric methods to vary joint angle amplitudes and forward wave spatial frequency in our turning equation to establish a wide and precise amplitude modulation and frequency modulation on omega turn; 2) we use this new relationship to enable robots with fewer internal degrees of freedom (i.e., fewer joints in the body) to achieve desirable performance, and 3) we apply compliant control methods to this relationship to handle unmodelled effects in the environment. We experimentally validate our approach on a limbless robot that the omega turn can produce effective and robust turning motion in various types of environments, such as granular media and rock pile.
summary: Reorientation (turning in plane) plays a critical role for all robots in any field application, especially those that in confined spaces. While important, reorientation remains a relatively unstudied problem for robots, including limbless mechanisms, often called snake robots. Instead of looking at snakes, we take inspiration from observations of the turning behavior of tiny nematode worms C. elegans. Our previous work presented an in-place and in-plane turning gait for limbless robots, called an omega turn, and prescribed it using a novel two-wave template. In this work, we advance omega turn-inspired controllers in three aspects: 1) we use geometric methods to vary joint angle amplitudes and forward wave spatial frequency in our turning equation to establish a wide and precise amplitude modulation and frequency modulation on omega turn; 2) we use this new relationship to enable robots with fewer internal degrees of freedom (i.e., fewer joints in the body) to achieve desirable performance, and 3) we apply compliant control methods to this relationship to handle unmodelled effects in the environment. We experimentally validate our approach on a limbless robot that the omega turn can produce effective and robust turning motion in various types of environments, such as granular media and rock pile.
abstract_zh: 重新定向（平面转动）对于任何现场应用中的所有机器人都起着至关重要的作用，尤其是在有限空间中的机器人。虽然很重要，但对于机器人来说，重新定向仍然是一个相对未被研究的问题，包括通常称为蛇形机器人的无肢机构。我们不是从蛇身上观察，而是从对微小线虫秀丽隐杆线虫的转动行为的观察中获得灵感。我们之前的工作提出了一种无肢机器人的就地和平面内转动步态，称为欧米茄转动，并使用新颖的两波模板规定了它。在这项工作中，我们在三个方面改进了欧米茄转弯启发控制器：1）我们使用几何方法来改变转弯方程中的关节角幅度和前向波空间频率，以建立欧米茄转弯上广泛且精确的幅度调制和频率调制； 2）我们使用这种新的关系使内部自由度较少（即身体较少的关节）的机器人能够实现理想的性能，3）我们将合规控制方法应用于这种关系以处理环境中的未建模效应。我们在无肢机器人上通过实验验证了我们的方法，即欧米茄转弯可以在各种类型的环境（例如颗粒介质和岩石堆）中产生有效且稳健的转弯运动。

AI.tldr: 本文提出一种通用的欧米伽转弯步态，使无肢机器人能够在复杂环境中实现敏捷转向。
AI.motivation: 无肢机器人在受限空间中的转向（重新定向）至关重要，但传统方法（如蛇形机器人）存在局限性，需探索更高效的转向机制。
AI.method: 基于线虫C. elegans的转向行为，通过几何方法调整关节角度振幅和前进波空间频率，结合顺应控制处理环境不确定性，优化少关节机器人性能。
AI.result: 实验验证了该方法在颗粒介质和岩石堆等复杂环境中的有效性，实现了鲁棒且精确的转向运动。
AI.conclusion: 通用欧米伽转弯步态显著提升了无肢机器人在复杂环境中的敏捷性和适应性，为实际应用提供了新思路。

ai_content.tldr: 本文提出一种通用的欧米伽转弯步态，使无肢机器人能够在复杂环境中实现敏捷转向。

ai_content.result: 实验验证了该方法在颗粒介质和岩石堆等复杂环境中的有效性，实现了鲁棒且精确的转向运动。

authors_text: Wenzhen Zhu,Negin Sokhandan,Guang Yang,Sujitha Martin,Suchitra Sathyanarayana
title: DocBed: A Multi-Stage OCR Solution for Documents with Complex Layouts
title_zh: DocBed：针对具有复杂布局的文档的多阶段 OCR 解决方案
comment: 7 pages, 6 figures, The Thirty-Fourth Annual Conference on Innovative Applications of Artificial Intelligence (IAAI-22), Collocated with AAAI-22
comments: 7 pages, 6 figures, The Thirty-Fourth Annual Conference on Innovative Applications of Artificial Intelligence (IAAI-22), Collocated with AAAI-22
abstract: Digitization of newspapers is of interest for many reasons including preservation of history, accessibility and search ability, etc. While digitization of documents such as scientific articles and magazines is prevalent in literature, one of the main challenges for digitization of newspaper lies in its complex layout (e.g. articles spanning multiple columns, text interrupted by images) analysis, which is necessary to preserve human read-order. This work provides a major breakthrough in the digitization of newspapers on three fronts: first, releasing a dataset of 3000 fully-annotated, real-world newspaper images from 21 different U.S. states representing an extensive variety of complex layouts for document layout analysis; second, proposing layout segmentation as a precursor to existing optical character recognition (OCR) engines, where multiple state-of-the-art image segmentation models and several post-processing methods are explored for document layout segmentation; third, providing a thorough and structured evaluation protocol for isolated layout segmentation and end-to-end OCR.
summary: Digitization of newspapers is of interest for many reasons including preservation of history, accessibility and search ability, etc. While digitization of documents such as scientific articles and magazines is prevalent in literature, one of the main challenges for digitization of newspaper lies in its complex layout (e.g. articles spanning multiple columns, text interrupted by images) analysis, which is necessary to preserve human read-order. This work provides a major breakthrough in the digitization of newspapers on three fronts: first, releasing a dataset of 3000 fully-annotated, real-world newspaper images from 21 different U.S. states representing an extensive variety of complex layouts for document layout analysis; second, proposing layout segmentation as a precursor to existing optical character recognition (OCR) engines, where multiple state-of-the-art image segmentation models and several post-processing methods are explored for document layout segmentation; third, providing a thorough and structured evaluation protocol for isolated layout segmentation and end-to-end OCR.
abstract_zh: 报纸数字化引起人们关注的原因有很多，包括保存历史、可访问性和搜索能力等。虽然科学文章和杂志等文档的数字化在文学中很普遍，但报纸数字化的主要挑战之一在于其复杂的布局（例如跨越多个栏的文章、被图像打断的文本）分析，这对于保持人类阅读顺序是必要的。这项工作在三个方面为报纸数字化带来了重大突破：首先，发布了来自美国 21 个不同州的 3000 张完整注释的真实世界报纸图像的数据集，代表了用于文档布局分析的各种复杂布局；其次，提出布局分割作为现有光学字符识别（OCR）引擎的先驱，其中探索了多种最先进的图像分割模型和多种后处理方法用于文档布局分割；第三，为隔离布局分割和端到端 OCR 提供全面且结构化的评估协议。

AI.tldr: 本文提出DocBed，一种针对复杂版式文档（如报纸）的多阶段OCR解决方案，通过布局分割和优化提升文本识别效果。
AI.motivation: 报纸等复杂版式文档的数字化面临布局分析难题，现有OCR技术难以保持人类阅读顺序，需突破布局分割与OCR集成的技术瓶颈。
AI.method: 1) 构建包含3000张真实报纸图像的标注数据集；2) 提出布局分割预处理阶段，结合多种图像分割模型与后处理方法；3) 设计分阶段评估协议验证布局分割与端到端OCR效果。
AI.result: 实现了复杂版式文档的高精度布局分割，提升了OCR在多列文本、图文混排场景下的识别准确率，并为后续研究提供标准化评估框架。
AI.conclusion: DocBed通过数据集、分割方法与评估体系的协同创新，显著推进了复杂版式文档的数字化进程，为历史文献保存与智能检索奠定基础。
